mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X3 20d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X3 20d engine typically costs £3,200–£5,800 supply only, with the B47 variants at the higher end. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. A snapped timing chain on an N47 can cost approximately £7,000 for a full engine replacement. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your X3.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X3 20d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which engine era your X3 20d has. The F25 (2010–2017) with the N47 has the well-documented rear-mounted timing chain risk — a known weakness that can cause catastrophic failure. The G01 (2017–2024) and G45 (2024–present) with the B47 are significantly more reliable — the chain was relocated to the front, fixing the N47's timing-chain issues. Both N47 and B47 share EGR cooler issues, subject to a BMW recall.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 20d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear on N47-engined cars (cold-start rattle is the warning sign), EGR cooler leaks affecting both N47 and B47 (subject to a BMW recall), and DPF clogging exacerbated by short journeys. The B47 is a significant improvement over the N47.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an X3 20d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and engine era. Against a well-kept G01/G45 worth £15,000–£25,000, a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 15–30% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired F25 worth £4,000–£8,000, the same recon may approach our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B47-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X3 20d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: F25 (2010–2017): N47D20C. G01 (2017–2024): B47D20A. G45 (2024–present): B47D20A/B. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£3,200–£5,500+ engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The B47 has far fewer timing chain issues — but regular oil changes remain essential.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X3 20d? Check These First
buyIf: * G01 (2017+) or G45 (2024+) with the B47 engine (significantly reduced chain risk)
* F25 with documented timing chain history (preventative replacement)
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
avoidIf: * F25 N47 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* No service history — the N47 is unforgiving of neglect
* Short-journey ownership history — DPF issues likely
* Unexplained coolant loss — EGR cooler may be failing

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X3 20d is BMW's best-selling diesel in the X3 range — a 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel that powers the F25, G01 and G45 generations. The F25 has the N47 — with the well-known rear-mounted chain risk. The G01 and G45 have the B47 — a significant improvement, fixing the chain design. Budget for chain maintenance if the history is unclear. With typical X3 values ranging from £6,000 (F25) to £35,000+ (G45), an engine replacement is a significant investment — the B47-engined G01/G45 is the safer choice.

cta.label: → Compare X3 20d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X3 20d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (N47 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N47 or B47) and oil-change discipline
* EGR Recall — Confirm recall completion with BMW (both N47 and B47 affected)
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
